description: Shared contract for all AI agents operating the Autopilot control plane

operatingDoctrine.objective: Operate Autopilot with maximum autonomy, reliability, traceability, security, observability, and continuity while minimizing local-machine dependency.
operatingDoctrine.localMachineRole: Control terminal only. Never the canonical source of memory, state, scheduling, logs, backups, or critical automation.
mandatoryPrinciples: Prefer cloud execution, scheduling, storage, logs, automation, and integrations whenever viable
Persist structural knowledge in GitHub, not in ephemeral local context
Treat local-only dependency as an exception that must be documented, justified, and reduced over time
Every important automation must be observable, auditable, and debuggable
Every meaningful change must consider rollback, retry, timeout, validation, and evidence of success
Every useful lesson must be institutionalized as documentation, workflow, template, contract, runbook, or explicit memory
analysisOrder: Locate existing workflows, docs, automation, and conventions
Identify local dependencies and fragility points
Identify what can move to cloud/GitHub
Assess risks, security, rollback, and observability
qualityGate: Preserve compatibility with the current flow
Provide a reversal or rollback path
Leave the project more autonomous than before

sessionGuard.description: Multi-agent concurrency control. Required when multiple agents share the same GitHub account.
protocol: Check if session-lock.json exists and is not expired
If locked by DIFFERENT agent, ABORT with error
If locked by SAME agent, refresh the lock
If expired or no lock, acquire new lock
Always release lock in the audit/cleanup stage

workspaceDiscovery.method: List directories under state/workspaces/ on autopilot-state branch
workspaceDiscovery.note: There is NO default workspace. Agents MUST identify the target workspace from conversation context before any operation.

workspaceIdentification.description: MANDATORY protocol for identifying which workspace to operate on. No workspace is default.
protocol: 1. BEFORE any operation, identify which company/workspace the user is talking about
2. Look for context clues: company name, repo names, stack mentions, tool mentions
3. If context is ambiguous, ASK the user which workspace before proceeding
4. NEVER assume a workspace — always confirm from context or ask
5. Once identified, verify workspace_id exists in state/workspaces/
6. Read workspace.json to get repos, token, and config for that workspace

rules: Cloud First, GitHub First, Local Last
NEVER assume a default workspace — always identify from conversation context or ask the user
Never store corporate secrets in the autopilot repo
Never store corporate code in the autopilot repo
Always use workspace_id, never hardcode tenant/org names
Always acquire lock before writing release state
Always release lock after operation completes or fails
Always write audit entry after any state mutation
Never use regex to edit YAML - use structured tooling
Never hardcode branch names - read from workspace.json
State is the source of truth, not agent memory
GitHub is the primary source of truth for project structure, workflows, docs, handoffs, and reusable operational knowledge
Never leave critical knowledge only on the local machine or only in session context
Always validate jq output with fallbacks (2>/dev/null || echo fallback)
Use base64 encoding when passing content between workflow jobs
Never silently swallow errors - always log before continuing
Never mix data, credentials, or operations between workspaces

errorHandling.jqCalls: Always add 2>/dev/null || echo 'fallback' to jq commands
errorHandling.apiCalls: Log error details before continuing or failing
errorHandling.lockRelease: Log warning if lock release fails, never silent || true
errorHandling.auditWrite: Retry once if audit write fails, then log error

changeRequirements: Document relevant operational changes in GitHub
Keep auditability and rollback in mind for every risky change
Prefer reusable workflows, templates, and runbooks over ad-hoc manual steps
Never introduce critical local-only automation if a GitHub/cloud path exists

multiAgentOrchestration.description: Universal agent system. Claude Code is primary. Any AI can be registered as backup/specialist via agent-registry.json.

agents._note: Additional agents registered in contracts/agent-registry.json. Legacy agents (copilot, codex) files preserved for history.

rules: NUNCA misturar dados entre empresas
Identificar workspace ANTES de qualquer acao
Cada empresa usa EXCLUSIVAMENTE seu proprio token
Se outro agente tem lock: ESPERAR ou criar handoff
Se Claude cair: Copilot assume em < 30 min
Spark dashboard mostra estado de TODAS as empresas
Memoria de cada agente e EXCLUSIVA — so ler a dos outros
